idea_summary: cuBLAS reference baseline
Initial adapted kernel from 16384x16384x16384 shape, using 128x128 tiles with K=
Larger tiles 256x128 with BLOCK_K=32 - actually worse due to register pressure
Smaller tiles 64x64 with BLOCK_K=32, num_stages=2 - even worse
Wider tiles 128x256 with K=64, stages=3 - worse
128x128 tiles with K=64, stages=2, threads=256 - more threads helps small K
128x128 tiles with K=64, stages=2, threads=128 - lower thread count better
128x128 tiles with K=64, stages=1, threads=128 - kernel hangs with single stage
stages=1 hangs
128x128 tiles with K=64, stages=3, threads=128 - massive improvement
stages=4 same as stages=3
Wider tiles 128x192 with K=64, stages=3 - excellent performance
Taller tiles 192x128 with K=64, stages=3 - slightly better than 128x192
192x192 tiles too large - worse than baseline
256x128 tiles too large in M dimension
BLOCK_K=32 worse than K=64
panel_size=4 slightly worse than 8
panel_size=16 similar to 8
stages=2 worse than stages=3 for 192x128
stages=4 also worse than stages=3 for 192x128
BLOCK_M=160 incompatible with WGMMA tile constraints
BLOCK_M=224 incompatible with WGMMA constraints
Narrower tiles 192x64 worse
256x64 tiles still worse than 192x128
